author: @slinkerdeer
text:
As someone who relies on food delivery too much, I agree. As a society, we have dropped the necessity to cook at home, and that's a bad thing. But this is a natural evolution of our interconnected society where, thanks to the systems we've built, we've made certain things easier. And the fact is, it's just easier to order in some food than cook it yourself. So whilst we have to acknowledge the bad things, we must acknowledge why they are the way they are. It's simply more efficient to just order food in than try and cook it yourself in the short term.

The solution is to teach our children long-term gratification, because that's what cooking your own food requires, that you appreciate long-term gratification.
